judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with essentially equivalent substance; Report 1 is marginally more rigorous (adds git cherry evidence and per-file scope verification) but both are comparable in correctness and completeness.

report_1.evidence: a PR stacked on it would orphan into merge conflicts or show a misleading diff. Every PR should be based on the trunk

report_2.evidence: With the dependency fully merged, there is no reason to stack; the PR should target main directly.

report_1.evidence: feat/pairwise-metric was cut from the tip of feat/weighted-scorer... landed via squash commit 1971f08 "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)". ... although it still exists on the remote at ac9d505, its content has already been merged into main

report_2.evidence: feat/pairwise-metric was created from the tip of feat/weighted-scorer... it was squash-merged into main. Evidence: main contains exactly one commit past the fork point — 1971f08 "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)"

report_1.evidence: scoring/scorer.py +13/−3: the entire flat-to-weighted scorer refactor... — not pairwise work at all; it is W1–W2 from feat/weighted-scorer... the branch's merge base with main predates the squash merge, so git's diff-from-merge-base re-shows the stacked foundation

report_2.evidence: scoring/scorer.py: 13 changed lines (rewrite from flat to weighted scoring...)... the entire scorer.py rewrite — is the feat/weighted-scorer work. It appears in the PR diff because the branch physically carries commits W1–W3, and the merge base (65204ad) predates the squash merge

report_1.evidence: Verify the result: git merge-base --is-ancestor main HEAD (branch now contains the current trunk tip); git log --oneline main..HEAD shows exactly 2 commits; git diff main...HEAD now equals the 5-line net-new diff — pairwise_preference() plus the grader-prompt line, nothing else.

report_2.evidence: Verify the result: git log --oneline main..feat/pairwise-metric should show exactly 2 commits, and git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric should now equal the 5-line pairwise-only delta; run the test suite.
